sections.heading: MATLAB Engine Overview
sections.content: The MATLAB Engine tab (tab_matlab.py) provides an optional high-precision stereo calibration pathway using MATLAB's Computer Vision Toolbox. MATLAB's calibration algorithms offer additional outlier rejection, tangential distortion modeling, and a visual diagnostic interface not available in the Python path. The tab launches a MATLAB session from within NEXUS, controls the Stereo Camera Calibrator App, and exports the result as a .mat file that can then be converted to a usable .npz via the DATA BRIDGE tab.

sections.heading: When to Use MATLAB Calibration
sections.content: The Python calibration tab (tab_pycalib.py) is sufficient for most use cases. Use the MATLAB path when:
list: Maximum precision required: MATLAB's calibration engine applies stronger outlier rejection and uses full rational distortion model (up to 3 radial + 2 tangential coefficients)
RMS > 0.5 px from Python calibration: MATLAB's visual diagnostics can identify and reject problematic image pairs
Existing MATLAB workflow: Your lab already uses MATLAB for calibration and you want to import existing .mat calibration files
Debugging distortion: MATLAB shows per-image and per-point reprojection error plots that help diagnose systematic problems

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.alertContent:
MATLAB requirements:
• MATLAB R2020a or later installed on the same PC as NEXUS
• Computer Vision Toolbox add-on installed (check via MATLAB Add-On Explorer)
• Python MATLAB Engine API installed: run pip install matlabengine — the version must match your MATLAB release exactly

If MATLAB is not installed, skip this tab and use PY CALIBRATION instead.

sections.heading: Checking MATLAB Engine Installation
sections.code:
# Verify MATLAB engine is importable:
import matlab.engine
print("MATLAB Engine: OK")

# If this throws ImportError:
# 1. Find your MATLAB installation: e.g. C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023b
# 2. In a CMD with admin rights, run:
#    cd "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023b\extern\engines\python"
#    python setup.py install
#
# Then re-run the import check.

# To list available MATLAB sessions:
names = matlab.engine.find_matlab()
print(f"Running MATLAB sessions: {names}")
# Output: ('MATLAB_7320',) if MATLAB is already open
# Output: () if no MATLAB session is running

sections.heading: MATLAB Engine Tab Workflow
steps: Ensure MATLAB is installed with the Computer Vision Toolbox
Go to the 🔴 MATLAB ENGINE tab
Click START MATLAB ENGINE — this launches a MATLAB session in the background (may take 20–40 seconds on first launch)
The status indicator turns green: MATLAB: CONNECTED
Click OPEN STEREO CALIBRATOR — this runs stereoCameraCalibrator inside the MATLAB session
In the MATLAB Stereo Camera Calibrator App: load your left and right image folders, set the square size, and run calibration
When satisfied with the results, click Export in the MATLAB app → choose Export Camera Parameters → save as a .mat file
Back in NEXUS, click STOP MATLAB ENGINE to cleanly shut down the MATLAB session
Go to the 🌐 DATA BRIDGE tab to convert the exported .mat file to stereo_calibration_bridge.npz

sections.heading: Starting the MATLAB Engine
Opening the Stereo Camera Calibrator
sections.code:
# From tab_matlab.py — launch calibrator inside MATLAB session
def open_stereo_calibrator(self):
    if self.eng is None:
        self.log("MATLAB engine not running — start it first")
        return

    # Run stereoCameraCalibrator in MATLAB
    # This opens the GUI app inside the MATLAB window
    self.eng.eval("stereoCameraCalibrator", nargout=0)
    self.log("Stereo Camera Calibrator opened in MATLAB")
    self.log("Load your left/right image folders, set square size, run calibration")
    self.log("When done: Export → Export Camera Parameters → save as .mat")

sections.heading: Stereo Camera Calibrator App — Guide
sections.content: Inside MATLAB's Stereo Camera Calibrator App, follow these steps:
list: Add Images: Click 'Add Images' → select your left camera folder → right camera folder. Images must have matching filenames (e.g., left_001.png ↔ right_001.png)
Set Square Size: Enter the exact square size in mm measured with calipers. This is the same value used in PY CALIBRATION
Detect: Click 'Detect' — MATLAB will find all chessboard corners and show coverage statistics
Calibrate: Click 'Calibrate' — MATLAB runs full stereo calibration and shows reprojection error plots
Remove Outliers: In the 'Mean Reprojection Error' bar chart, click high-error bars to select and remove outlier image pairs. Re-run calibration after removal
Check Accuracy: Target Mean Error < 0.5 px in MATLAB for best results in NEXUS
Export: Click 'Export Camera Parameters' → saves a stereoParams variable to a .mat file

sections.heading: Recommended Image Set for MATLAB
rows: Minimum image pairs after outlier removal
Same as Python path — spatial coverage is critical
Avoid JPEG — compression artifacts can affect corner detection
e.g., img001.png in both left/ and right/ directories

sections.heading: Stopping the MATLAB Engine
After Exporting from MATLAB
sections.content: Once you have saved the .mat file from MATLAB's Stereo Camera Calibrator, you must convert it to a Python-compatible .npz file before NEXUS can use it. This conversion is handled entirely by the DATA BRIDGE tab — NEXUS never reads .mat files directly from the engine.
sections.alertContent: After conversion, go to Settings and update the STEREO_FILE path to point to data/stereo_calibration_bridge.npz. Then restart the engine to apply the MATLAB-precision calibration.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting MATLAB Tab
rows: Run: pip install matlabengine (must match MATLAB version)
MATLAB license server delay or first-launch JVM startup
Wait up to 2 minutes; kill and retry if it never completes
Open MATLAB → Add-Ons → search 'Computer Vision Toolbox' → Install
Engine connects but calibrator doesn't open
MATLAB desktop is not in focus or is minimized
Bring MATLAB window to foreground after clicking Open Calibrator
Safe to ignore — set self.eng = None and update status label
